title: DIY Galvanized Tub Vertical Planter
summary: Adding plants to your front entry space can help to create a warm and inviting porch.
description: Adding plants to your front entry space can help to create a warm and inviting porch. But when you have a small porch, like I do, space is often limited so creating these vertical planters out of galvanized tubs is a great way to add stylish planter shelves to a small space.

steps.title: Mark the Tub
steps.body:
*These plans are to build (1) galvanized tub vertical planter.*
We used painters tape to mark where we wanted to cut our galvanized tub. We chose to cut it along a indentation in the tub, but were careful to make sure that it was wide enough to accommodate our wood.

steps.title: Cut the Tub
steps.body: We used a hand-held cut off tool with a metal cutting blade and cut our tub where we marked it off. We wore leather gloves and protective gear while doing this.

steps.title: Sand the Edges of the Tub
steps.body: We used a circular sanding wheel attached to an electric drill to sand the edges of the tub where it was cut.

steps.title: Cut Wood
steps.body:
We cut our wood using a compound miter saw. We cut 2 lengths of wood to 16.75" with both ends cut at a 10 degree inward angle. This was to match the inside taper of the tub.
The other 2 lengths of wood were cut to a 14.5" length with no angle.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: We used a Kreg Jig to drill pocket holes into both ends of the 14.5" length pieces of wood.

steps.title: Join Wood Pieces
steps.body: Once our pocket holes were done, we used Kreg Screws, the Kreg drill bit and a drill to join the wood pieces together. A right angle clamp was really helpful when we did this.

steps.title: Attach L Bracket
steps.body: On four corners of our wood box, we attached L brackets using a drill and screws.

steps.title: Bend L Brackets
steps.body: We then bent our L brackets to fit the inside angle of our tub using a hammer.

steps.title: Drill L Bracket Hole
steps.body: We used a drill to make the holes in the L brackets a bit larger to accommodate the rivet.

steps.title: Mark Rivet Holes
steps.body: We placed the wood box into the tub and marked where the rivet holes would need to be drilled with a permanent marker.

steps.title: Drill Rivet Holes
steps.body: We removed the box from our tub and drilled holes where we marked for our rivets.

steps.title: Place Rivet
steps.body: We inserted the rivet into our pre-drilled hole and into the L brackets on the wood shelf.

steps.title: Rivet Shelf to Tub
steps.body: Then we used a hand rivet tool to secure the tub to the L bracket.

steps.title: Hang Vertical Planter
steps.body: We used special vinyl siding hooks to attach our vertical planters to our patio wall.

steps.title: Pot Plants
steps.body: We then picked plants that we thought would be nice to go on the planters. We picked out some beautiful succulents and a cactus and potted them. But flowers or herbs would also be really lovely.

steps.title: Add Plants to Shelf
steps.body: Then we placed our potted plants onto our shelf and we were ready to enjoy our newly updated porch decor!
